draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left navigation over tab-switching in an AI workspace
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: Explore fixed sidebars, collapsible panels, persistent tool access
facade.rejectImplies: Test tab-based navigation, modal dialogs, space-maximizing layouts

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left navigation over tab-switching in an AI workspace
evidence.implication: Explore fixed sidebars, collapsible panels, persistent tool access

facade.hypothesis: The workspace adopts a chat-like, approachable voice rather than corporate formality
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Users expect casual language, supportive messaging, and personality in UI copy and interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer polished, minimal, business-standard language that stays out of the way

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high-contrast layouts with a dark navigation zone anchoring focus on the bright content area
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light canvas
facade.acceptImplies: Probe darker navigation patterns, contained palettes, strong figure-ground separation
facade.rejectImplies: Explore softer contrast, monochromatic schemes, or reversed (light nav + dark content)

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer smooth material transitions over instant state changes in interactive elements
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid vs snappy — whether micro-interactions feel animated or instantaneous
facade.label: Buttons fade in
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use easing curves and opacity transitions for feedback; buttons should breathe rather than snap
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should use instant, crisp state changes; buttons should toggle without animation overhead

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, soft-focus interfaces that emphasize ambient lighting and depth over hard contrast
facade.label: Glowing edges
facade.acceptImplies: Explore neon halos, soft shadows, high-key backlighting, luminous separators, glow effects on interactive elements
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward crisp high-contrast typography, flat planes, sharp borders, cool color temps, minimal glow or blur

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer information-dense layouts with multiple metrics visible at once over minimalist single-metric views
facade.axisTargeted: Data density: comprehensive overview vs. focused simplicity
facade.label: Dense dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should support power-user workflows with layered information architecture, dashboard-style panels, and high information scent per screen
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should prioritize clarity and cognitive load reduction, showing one primary metric or action per view with progressive disclosure

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer modular card-based layouts over dense table-like arrangements for scanning multiple AI outputs or tasks
facade.axisTargeted: information density and visual modularity vs. compact tabular display
facade.label: Cards in a list
facade.acceptImplies: Explore card grids, whitespace-forward designs, one-item-per-card patterns; assume users value skimmability over row-column data tables
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer compact, data-dense layouts like spreadsheets or lists; cards could feel wasteful in an AI workspace where efficiency matters
